viewData.name: Peformance Measure Categories to Business Process
Cost Model
viewData.description: Allows you to capture costs for elements in the model.  Start date and end data are optional, but if you do not set them, the cost will be considered to be ongoing.

nodes.name: Cost Component (Ad Hoc, Annual, Monthly, Quarterly)

viewData.name: Application Dependency
viewData.description: Modelling of application depenedencies.  Remember you are modelling dependencies here, not flow.  The flow is the dependency reversed.  So the source applications/APIs (on the right of the diagram) will send data to the applications target (on the left of the diagram)

viewData.description: Technologies supporting an application

viewData.description: Deployments of an application and the nodes/servers they are hosted on

viewData.name: strategicPlans
viewData.description: Allows you to map your roadmaps to strategic plans, in turn mapping those to the projects implementing them.  Additionally, you can map the changes the projects are making and you can also map projects into programmes

edges.label: static_architecture_of_app_provider
edges.description: Static architecture belongs to source application (inverse)

edges.label: contained_in_static_application_provider_architecture
edges.description: Static architecture contains source usage node (inverse direction on slot)
edges.note: Slot is stored on the usage node pointing to architecture

edges.label: contained_in_static_application_provider_architecture
edges.description: Source usage node contained in static architecture

edges.label: static_usage_of_app_provider
edges.description: Source usage node references source application

edges.label: :FROM
edges.description: Integration relation originates from source usage node
edges.note: Neo4j relationship type without ns0__ prefix

edges.label: :TO
edges.description: Integration relation targets destination usage node
edges.note: Neo4j relationship type without ns0__ prefix

edges.label: apu_to_apu_relation_inforeps
edges.description: Integration relation links to information exchange relations

edges.label: contract_component_to_element
edges.description: Integration relation can reference interface/API used
edges.note: This relationship name appears in the integration_relations config

edges.label: atire_app_pro_to_inforep
edges.description: Exchange relation back to APU-APU relation (inverse)
edges.note: Enables finding integration from info exchange perspective

edges.label: atire_app_pro_to_inforep
edges.description: Exchange relation links to underlying app-inforep relation

edges.label: atire_acquisition_method
edges.description: How data is acquired (Batch, Real-time, API, Manual, etc.)

edges.label: app_pro_to_inforep_to_inforep
edges.description: App-InfoRep relation points to the information representation

edges.label: contained_in_static_application_provider_architecture
edges.description: Target usage node contained in target static architecture

edges.label: contained_in_static_application_provider_architecture
edges.description: Target static architecture contains target usage node (inverse)

edges.label: static_usage_of_app_provider
edges.description: Target usage node references target application

edges.label: static_architecture_of_app_provider
edges.description: Static architecture belongs to target application (inverse)
